intro: Homalium guianense is a plant species belonging to the family Salicaceae, commonly known as the willow family, and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Homalium guianense include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which have been reported to exhibit potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Homalium guianense is often used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
